repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

On Windows, `:!echo тест` shows `????` because the console code page defaults to a legacy ANSI encoding (e.g. CP1252) instead of `UTF-8`

Closes #33480

## Solution

Call `SetConsoleOutputCP(CP_UTF8)` and `SetConsoleCP(CP_UTF8)` in `do_os_system()` before spawning child processes, and restore the original values after. It covers both `:!` and `system()` since they both go through `do_os_system()`

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
      it('with shell=cmd.exe, spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage', function()
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -166,6 +166,11 @@ describe('system()', function()
         test_shell_unquoting()
       end)
 
+      it('cmd.exe with UTF-8', function()

comment_data.body:
actually, why does the test mention cmd.exe? that's used to test the functionality, but the functionality applies to all shells, right?
```suggestion
      it('spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage', function()
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -166,6 +166,11 @@ describe('system()', function()
         test_shell_unquoting()
       end)
 
+      it('with shell=cmd.exe, spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage', function()

comment_data.body: does this test definitely fail before the change you made? i thought we had tests like this already.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -166,6 +166,11 @@ describe('system()', function()
         test_shell_unquoting()
       end)
 
+      it('with shell=cmd.exe, spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage', function()
+        command('set shell=cmd.exe')
+        eq('тест\n', eval([[system('echo тест')]]))

comment_data.body:
yeah it did fail before:

```bash
FAILED   system() executes shell function with shell=cmd.exe, 
          spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage

 Expected: 'тест\n'
 Passed in: '????\n'
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -166,6 +166,11 @@ describe('system()', function()
         test_shell_unquoting()
       end)
 
+      it('with shell=cmd.exe, spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage', function()
+        command('set shell=cmd.exe')
+        eq('тест\n', eval([[system('echo тест')]]))

comment_data.body:
haha oh yeah lol

I was tunnel visioning on `cmd.exe` since that's what I used to reproduce it

Fixed!
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -166,6 +166,11 @@ describe('system()', function()
         test_shell_unquoting()
       end)
 
+      it('with shell=cmd.exe, spawns child process with UTF-8 codepage', function()

comment_regular_data.body:
Is this something needed for `jobstart()` also? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/9705a1c13b04c20a6b8eb1f57d543f4d08240876/src/nvim/eval/funcs.c#L3479

(Not to mention `vim.system()` @lewis6991 )
btw, I'm not really sure I can say for certain if we need to change anything on `jobstart()` 😞

title: fix(windows): set console code page to UTF-8 for shell commands
